title: Atlas Stream Processing Access and Auditing

sections.heading: Organization and Project Access
sections.body:
Users manage stream processing workspaces and their associated connection registries at
the project level. Atlas Stream Processing provides the [Project Stream Processing Owner](<Project Stream Processing Owner>) role for this purpose. A user with this role can
perform any [Stream Processing Workspaces](atlas-sp-manage-spi) action, and manage the
databases and database users within the project. Assign this role to a
user to enable them to perform all the actions necessary to configure
an Atlas Stream Processing project without granting any unnecessary permissions
to features in accordance with the principle of least privilege.

If necessary, you can perform any of the actions authorized by the
`Project Stream Processing Owner` role as a user with either the
`Project Owner` or `Organization Owner` roles.

sections.heading: Stream Processing Workspace Access
sections.body:
You can access an existing stream processing workspace and [manage stream processors](streams-manage-processor) as a [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users),
analogous to how you access an Atlas cluster. The roles and
actions assigned to your database users determine which operations
they can perform on stream processors within a stream processing workspace. Atlas Stream Processing
provides the following privilege actions:

- `processStreamProcessor`

- `createStreamProcessor`

- `startStreamProcessor`

- `stopStreamProcessor`

- `dropStreamProcessor`

- `listStreamProcessors`

- `sampleStreamProcessor`

- `streamProcessorStats`

- `listConnections`

You can assign exactly those privilege actions to a database user or
[custom roles](mongodb-roles) that you need. Alternatively, a
database user with the [atlasAdmin](manual/reference/built-in-roles) or
[readWriteAnyDatabase](manual/reference/built-in-roles) can perform all of these actions.

sections.heading: Networking
sections.body:
Atlas Stream Processing supports connections to both Atlas clusters and
external streaming data sources such as [Apache Kafka](https://kafka.apache.org/). Connections within
Atlas do not require manual configuration. To connect to an
external streaming data source, you must add Atlas IP addresses
to your external streaming data source's access list.

To identify the necessary Atlas IP addresses, run the following
command:

```sh
curl -H 'Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2023-11-15+json' -s \
'https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/unauth/controlPlaneIPAddresses'
```

This returns a list of available Atlas IP addresses, grouped by
provider and region. Identify all outbound IP addresses for the
provider-region pair in which the target stream processing workspace is deployed, and add
these to your external data source's access list. To learn more, see
[Atlas IP addresses](atlas-add-inbound-ips).

When you configure a connection to an external streaming data source,
you can choose between access over public IP addresses, or over a
[VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering) connection. To learn more, see
[Add a Connection to the Connection Registry](atlas-sp-manage-connection-add).

sections.heading: Restricting Access to Specific stream processing workspaces
sections.body:
By default, a user with Atlas Stream Processing privilege actions can perform the
associated operations on all stream processing workspaces. You can restrict the
application of these privileges to specific stream processing workspaces.

sections.heading: Step 1
sections.body:
Click **Edit** in the row of the user whose
permissions you want to modify.

sections.heading: Step 2
sections.body:
In the modal window, toggle the switch labeled
**Restrict Access to Specific Clusters
/Federated Database Instances/Stream Processing Workspaces** on.

sections.heading: Step 3
sections.body:
Find the names of the stream processing workspaces for which you want to grant
the user privileges. Check the box next to a name to grant the
user privileges for that stream processing workspace. Uncheck the box to deny the user
privileges for that stream processing workspace

sections.heading: Execution Profiles
sections.body:
You can configure the database user role that you use when connecting
to an Atlas database as either a [$source](pipe.$source) or a
[$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) sink. This allows you to prevent
Atlas Stream Processing-specific database users from gaining indirect access to
the cluster hosting that database through the credentials of the
elevated-privilege Atlas user that configures the stream processing workspace and its
connections.

> **Note:** In accordance with the principle of least privilege, define a
> custom role with only those privileges a user needs to perform
> their desired operations.

sections.heading: Step 1
sections.body:
In the pane of the stream processing workspace you want to configure, click
**Manage**.

sections.heading: Step 2
sections.body:
Click the **Connection Registry** tab. In the row
of the Atlas database connection you want to configure, click
pencil-alt.

sections.heading: Step 3
sections.body:
From the **Execute As** drop-down menu, select the
role to use when connecting to the database.

sections.heading: Auditing
sections.body:
Atlas Stream Processing auditing lets administrators track authentication and
entity management events on their stream processing workspaces. Each time an auditable
event occurs on a given stream processing workspace, Atlas Stream Processing writes that event to the
log for that stream processing workspace. A log persists for the lifetime of the stream processing workspace
to which it belongs, and Atlas Stream Processing never truncates old events. If
you delete a stream processing workspace, the log belonging to that stream processing workspace persists for
an additional 30 days.

To download audit logs for your stream processing workspace, see [Download Audit Logs](atlas-sp-manage-spi-audit).

Atlas Stream Processing supports auditing the following authentication events:

| Event                     | Description                                                               |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Successful Authentication | Occurs when a user successfully connects to a stream processing workspace |
| Failed Authentication     | Occurs when a user fails to connect to a stream processing workspace      |
| Connection Termination    | Occurs when a user closes their connection                                |

Atlas Stream Processing supports auditing the following entity management events:

| startStreamProcessor  | Occurs when a user starts a stream processor          |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| createStreamProcessor | Occurs when a user creates a stream processor         |
| stopStreamProcessor   | Occurs when a user stops a stream processor           |
| dropStreamProcessor   | Occurs when a user deletes a stream processor         |
| .process()            | Occurs when a user issues a .process() call           |
| .sample()             | Occurs when a user issues a .sample() call            |
| Audit log accessed    | Occurs when a user attempts to download the audit log |
